target_semantics_note: Target is the sum of citation counts from counts_by_year for years publication_year through publication_year + 2 (inclusive; horizon_years+1 calendar years when including publication year). Calendar-year granularity, not exact month-level windows.

eligibility_summary.eligibility_cutoff_description: Row eligible iff publication_year + horizon_years <= max_available_citation_year (max_available_citation_year=2026 from observed counts_by_year in this dataset).

counts_by_year_handling_note: Missing or empty counts_by_year_json is explicitly treated as a zero yearly-count series: each year in the horizon contributes 0, so the target value for such rows is 0. This is documented in features/targets.py (_parse_counts_by_year, compute_calendar_horizon_target).
note_dataset_vs_target: Dataset-level cited_by_count (in dataset_profile) is the current cumulative citation count from the snapshot. The calendar-horizon target is the sum of citations over a fixed number of calendar years from counts_by_year. They are different: do not confuse dataset citation distribution with the actual modeling target distribution reported here.
